frameworks.name: model-based planning
frameworks.one_line: agents plan over an internal task model to choose actions

frameworks.name: representational abstraction
frameworks.one_line: task representations as propositional rules over objects and relational categories

frameworks.name: cross-domain transfer of solutions
frameworks.one_line: prior accounts emphasizing reuse of solutions from previously learned domains

core_question: What form of task representation supports flexible within-domain generalization in human problem-solving?
key_assumptions: the same planning framework can yield different behaviors under different task representations
object- and relation-based propositional rules are candidate representations
manipulations: class of task representation provided to the planning agent
variation of within-domain task structure across novel grid-based video games
agent vs. human comparison across the task series
key_findings: the same model-based planner produces distinct generalization behaviors under different task representations
agents with object-and-relation propositional rule representations align with human behavior on novel grid tasks
interpretations: within-domain flexibility in humans derives from propositional rule representations over objects and relational categories
representational abstraction, not just planning machinery, is central to human-like generalization
